chapter_summary: Genesis 1 is the very first chapter of the whole Bible. It tells us how God made everything — light and darkness, sky and sea, land and plants, sun and moon, fish and birds and animals — and last of all, people! Every time God made something He said, 'It is good.' On the sixth day God made people in His very own image. Then He looked at everything He had made and said it was very good. This chapter shows us that God is powerful, creative, and that the world He made is a wonderful gift.
main_lesson: God made everything — and it is all very good! You were made by God, in His image.
memory_verse: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
parent_guide: Genesis 1 is foundational theology for young children. Key themes: (1) Creation ex nihilo — God made everything from nothing, out of the power of His word alone. Help children see that this means God is unlike anything else in creation. (2) The repeated refrain 'it was good' — creation is not an accident or a mistake. It is intentional, beautiful, and valued by God. (3) The image of God (v.27) — every human being carries the imago Dei. This is the basis for human dignity, kindness to others, and why every person matters. For young children: you can explore creativity together — God made the world, and when we make things, draw things, or care for things, we are reflecting His image.
application_for_children: Go outside this week and notice something beautiful God made. Say 'thank You, God' for it. Draw or make something creative as a family — and remember: when you create, you are reflecting the God who made everything.

verses.kjv: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
verses.little_bible: Before anything existed — before the world, before stars, before time — God was there. And He made everything.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: At the very start, God made everything.
verses.meaning: God is the Creator of all things. Nothing exists that He did not make. He was there before the beginning — and He made it all.
verses.prayer: God, You are amazing. You made the whole world from nothing. Thank You!
verses.discussion_question: Can you imagine a time when there was nothing at all? Who was there?
verses.family_discussion: What is the most amazing thing God made that you can think of?
verses.do_it_today: Go outside and look at the sky. Say out loud: 'God made this!'
verses.illustration_prompt: Deep, dark space with a single bright light beginning to appear as God speaks — swirling stars and galaxies forming

verses.kjv: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.
verses.little_bible: God spoke into the darkness and said, 'Let there be light!' — and light burst into the world.
verses.meaning: God is so powerful that He creates just by speaking. His words are full of power — and He still speaks today through the Bible.
verses.prayer: God, Your words are powerful. Help me listen when You speak through the Bible.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the very first light looked like?
verses.family_discussion: How is God speaking still today? How do we hear Him?
verses.do_it_today: Turn off the lights for one minute, then turn them on. Think about what God did on the first day.
verses.illustration_prompt: An explosion of brilliant warm golden light bursting through darkness, like the very first sunrise

verses.kjv: And God called the dry land Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that it was good.
verses.little_bible: God made the dry land and the great seas. He looked at what He had made and said, 'This is good!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made the land and the sea. He said it was good.
verses.meaning: God is the one who named and shaped the world. And He called it good — creation is a gift to be enjoyed and cared for.
verses.memory_phrase: God made it, and it is good.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for the land and the sea and all the beautiful places You made.
verses.discussion_question: What is your favourite place in creation — the mountains, the sea, a forest, a garden?
verses.family_discussion: How can we take care of the earth God called 'good'?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing to care for the earth today — water a plant, pick up litter, or go outside and enjoy what God made.
verses.illustration_prompt: Sweeping ocean waves meeting a green rocky shoreline under a bright blue sky, full of wonder and beauty

verses.kjv: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.
verses.little_bible: God made the sun to shine during the day and the moon to glow at night. And He scattered billions of stars across the sky.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made the sun, moon, and all the stars.
verses.meaning: Every sunrise and every starry night is God's handiwork — a daily reminder that He is the Creator and His world is full of wonder.
verses.memory_phrase: God made the sun, moon, and stars.
verses.prayer: God, every morning when I see the sun and every night when I see the stars, remind me that You made them.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen the stars at night? How many do you think there are?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think God made something as beautiful as a starry sky?
verses.do_it_today: Tonight, look up at the sky — sun, clouds, or stars — and thank God for making it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A vast starry night sky over a sleeping world, with the moon glowing gently — breathtakingly beautiful

verses.kjv: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.
verses.little_bible: God made people in His own image — He made us to be like Him. He made boys and girls, men and women — all in His image.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made people to be like Him. That means you!
verses.meaning: Every person — every single one — is made in God's image. That is why every person matters, has value, and deserves to be treated with love.
verses.memory_phrase: I am made in God's image.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for making me in Your image. Help me see Your image in other people too.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think it means to be made in God's image?
verses.family_discussion: If every person is made in God's image, how should that change the way we treat people?
verses.do_it_today: Look in a mirror and say: 'I am made in God's image.' Then go and show kindness to someone else who is too.
verses.illustration_prompt: God's hands lovingly shaping a person from the earth, with light and warmth all around, the person opening their eyes

verses.kjv: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, it was very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.
verses.little_bible: God looked at everything He had made — the whole world, all the animals, and people — and said, 'This is VERY good!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God looked at everything and said it was very, very good.
verses.meaning: Creation was not just good — it was very good. God delighted in what He had made. He delights in you too.
verses.memory_phrase: God made me, and I am very good.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You made me and called me very good. Help me live in a way that makes You glad.
verses.discussion_question: How does it feel to know that God looked at you and said 'very good'?
verses.family_discussion: As a family, go around and say what you see that is 'very good' in each other — as God made you.
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone in your family one thing that is 'very good' about them, because God made them.
verses.illustration_prompt: God looking out over the beautiful completed creation — oceans, forests, animals, and people — smiling with deep delight
